[[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Shirazusou Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Shirazusou Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Shirazusou Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Shirazusou Shrine |type=point|id=Qqq544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Shirazusou Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Shirazusou Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=qq570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|qq15 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] revision.text:
[[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Shirazusou Shrine |type=point|id=Qqq544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Shirazusou Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Shirazusou Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=qq570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|qq15 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Shirazusou Shrine |type=point|id=Qqq544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Shirazusou Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Shirazusou Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=qq570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|qq15 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Shirazusou Shrine |type=point|id=Qqq544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Shirazusou Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Shirazusou Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=qq570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|qq15 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Qqq544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=qq570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|qq15 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3年}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year }} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' - Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7年}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year }}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year }} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year }}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year }} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year }}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘|12=draft}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|12=draft}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year|12=draft}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|12=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|12=draft}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|12=draft}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]
