Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘|12=draft}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|12=draft}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year|12=draft}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|12=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|12=draft}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|12=draft}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|12=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘|12=draft}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|12=draft}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year|12=draft}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|12=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|12=draft}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|12=draft}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|12=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|12=draft}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|12=draft}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:櫛田宮 up to rev 102135443
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 櫛田宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 佐賀県神埼市神埼町神埼419番地1 |ISO = JP-41 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 36.6 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 22|経度秒 = 21.3 |ラベル位置 = bottom |祭神 = 櫛田三神 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = [[景行天皇]]御宇 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 4月7・8日（春祭）
10月7・8日（秋祭） |神事 = みゆき大祭（隔年4月第一土・日曜日） }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[神埼荘]]の三所大明神
1.櫛田宮、2.高志神社、3.白角折神社 |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=櫛田宮 |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=高志神社 |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=白角折神社}} '''櫛田宮'''（くしだぐう）は、[[佐賀県]][[神埼市]]に所在する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。博多の[[櫛田神社 (福岡市)|櫛田神社]]はこの分社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[景行天皇]]（在位は4世紀前半と推定される）が当地に巡幸した折に不幸が続き住人が苦しんでいると聞き、当社を建て神を祀り鎮めたのが発祥という[[#角川|『角川日本地名大辞典』]]、「櫛田宮」頁。（参考:[https://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7216783 JLogos]）。 その後は災厄もなくなったことからこの地を「神幸（かむさき）」と称し現在の[[神埼郡|神埼]]（かんざき）の地名の起こりとなったと伝えられている{{R|角川1}}。 [[元寇]]の際には、櫛田宮の霊剣を福岡の[[櫛田神社 (福岡市)|櫛田神社]]に移して祈祷をし、その霊験で元軍は撤退したという話が伝わる{{R|角川1}}。なお、櫛田神社は櫛田宮からの分祀により創建されたものである。 [[神埼荘]]の鎮守として[[高志神社]]神埼市千代田町下板および[[白角折神社]]とともに三所大明神、三社宮と称された[[#角川|『角川日本地名大辞典』]]、「神埼郡」頁。。現代の「みゆき大祭」でもこの3社と櫛森・櫛丸の櫛田宮境内2社の[[神輿]]が奉納される{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=みゆき大祭 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 [[永久 (元号)|永久]]3年（[[1115年]]）に[[鳥羽天皇]]の勅で社殿を造営したとき勅使として下向した本告道景と伴兼直は当地に移り住み、それぞれ後に武士化した本告氏および執行氏の祖となって、両家は櫛田宮を含む三所大明神の宮司を務めたという{{R|角川1}}。 長く神埼一帯の総鎮守として崇敬されており、当社で保管されてきた古文書には平安時代後期の12世紀以降、度々朝廷や幕府、地元領主による造修が行われたことが記されている{{R|角川1}}。江戸時代には[[長崎街道]]が整備され、神埼宿が当社周辺に設けられた。 明治4年（1871年）に郷社、1880年（明治13年）に県社に列されている{{R|角川1}}。 == 祭神 == : 「櫛田三神」と称される。 * [[スサノオ|須佐之男命]] * [[クシナダヒメ|櫛稲田姫命]] * [[日本武尊|日本武命]] === 境内社 === * 櫛森稲荷神社 * 櫛丸稲荷神社 * [[祇園社]] * [[弁財天]]社 * [[天満宮]] == 主な祭事 == * 春祭 - [[4月7日]]・[[4月8日|8日]] ** 隔年で「みゆき大祭」と称し大規模に斎行される（西暦末尾偶数年の4月第一土・日曜日。[[2010年]]は[[4月3日]]・[[4月4日|4日]]）。御神幸や神楽奉納等が行われる。 * 秋祭 - [[10月7日]]・[[10月8日|8日]] == 文化財等 == '''佐賀県指定重要文化財'''（県指定有形文化財） * 石造肥前鳥居 - 柱と貫が3本継で慶長7年（1602）造立の銘がある初期の[[肥前鳥居]]。高さ3.35 m、笠木の長さ4.45 m。鍋島信濃守（[[鍋島勝茂]]）の記名がある。1964年5月23日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html#k_ju024 |title=県指定（建造物の部）01 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2021-08-19 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 '''佐賀県指定重要有形民俗文化財'''（県指定有形民俗文化財） * 御神幸祭[[絵馬]]（拝殿内） - 獅子舞（太神楽）を先頭に4基の神輿と行列が練り歩く神幸祭の様子を描いたもので、縦159cm・横184cmの大絵馬。「[[安政]]五戌午年八月吉祥日」の日付記載がある。1979年3月31日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html#k_yumi06 |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）01 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2020-06-23 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 '''佐賀県指定重要民俗文化財'''（県指定民俗文化財） * [[太神楽]]（だいかぐら） - 神幸祭（みゆき大祭）の「先払い」として行列の先頭で供奉される[[獅子舞]]。尾崎地区の住民によって行われ、現在は尾崎西分と尾崎東分で交互に担当している。獅子は緑色で長く30人ほどが入って交代しながら行う。垂れるほど目一杯の切紙で作った頭髪をまとい、道行しながら家々を廻る荒獅子では頭を振って邪気を払う。落ちた頭髪は安産や魔除けのご利益があるとされる。ほかに「もうし」、「ササラ」、太鼓、笛など総勢150名以上が出演する。1966年4月23日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html#k_mumi12 |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2020-06-23 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmCommunityPeopleDetail02.aspx |title=みゆき大祭 尾崎西分の太神楽 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |date=2012-03-30 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 '''神埼市指定重要文化財'''（市指定有形文化財） * 櫛田宮文書{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmAboutDetail3.aspx |title=神埼市の指定文化財 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2024-03-02}} - 「櫛田神社文書」として『佐賀県史料集成』古文書編第5巻（佐賀県立図書館、1960年）に収録 '''神埼市指定重要無形文化財'''（市指定無形文化財） * 締元行列 - みゆき大祭の御神幸に随従する。 '''記念物・樹木''' * 琴の楠 - 景行天皇が巡行の折に埋めた琴が[[クスノキ|楠]]になった伝説のある神木で、7回半周回すると「琴の音色が聞こえる」とされる{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=櫛田宮（くしだぐう） |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |date=2012-03-13 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。推定樹齢1000年。これを含めて境内には「佐賀県名木・古木」に登録されているクスノキが3本、[[イチョウ]]が1本あり、うちクスノキ1本とイチョウが推定樹齢700年を数える{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=佐賀県名木・古木 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2023-06-14 |accessdate=2023-08-10}}。 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[長崎本線]][[神埼駅]]下車、南西方向へ徒歩約10分。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} == 関連項目 == * [[櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] * [[吉野ヶ里遺跡]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ 櫛田宮ホームページ]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=佐賀県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたくう}} [[Category:佐賀県の神社]] [[Category:佐賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:神埼市の歴史]] [[Category:佐賀県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:神埼市の建築物]] [[Category:景行天皇]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:佐賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[櫛田宮]] to [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 櫛田宮 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 佐賀県神埼市神埼町神埼419番地1 |ISO = JP-41 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 36.6 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 22|経度秒 = 21.3 |ラベル位置 = bottom |祭神 = 櫛田三神 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = [[景行天皇]]御宇 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 4月7・8日（春祭）
10月7・8日（秋祭） |神事 = みゆき大祭（隔年4月第一土・日曜日） }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[神埼荘]]の三所大明神
1.櫛田宮、2.高志神社、3.白角折神社 |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=櫛田宮 |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=高志神社 |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=白角折神社}} '''櫛田宮'''（くしだぐう）は、[[佐賀県]][[神埼市]]に所在する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。博多の[[櫛田神社 (福岡市)|櫛田神社]]はこの分社。 == 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[景行天皇]]（在位は4世紀前半と推定される）が当地に巡幸した折に不幸が続き住人が苦しんでいると聞き、当社を建て神を祀り鎮めたのが発祥という[[#角川|『角川日本地名大辞典』]]、「櫛田宮」頁。（参考:[https://jlogos.com/docomosp/word.html?id=7216783 JLogos]）。 その後は災厄もなくなったことからこの地を「神幸（かむさき）」と称し現在の[[神埼郡|神埼]]（かんざき）の地名の起こりとなったと伝えられている{{R|角川1}}。 [[元寇]]の際には、櫛田宮の霊剣を福岡の[[櫛田神社 (福岡市)|櫛田神社]]に移して祈祷をし、その霊験で元軍は撤退したという話が伝わる{{R|角川1}}。なお、櫛田神社は櫛田宮からの分祀により創建されたものである。 [[神埼荘]]の鎮守として[[高志神社]]神埼市千代田町下板および[[白角折神社]]とともに三所大明神、三社宮と称された[[#角川|『角川日本地名大辞典』]]、「神埼郡」頁。。現代の「みゆき大祭」でもこの3社と櫛森・櫛丸の櫛田宮境内2社の[[神輿]]が奉納される{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=みゆき大祭 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 [[永久 (元号)|永久]]3年（[[1115年]]）に[[鳥羽天皇]]の勅で社殿を造営したとき勅使として下向した本告道景と伴兼直は当地に移り住み、それぞれ後に武士化した本告氏および執行氏の祖となって、両家は櫛田宮を含む三所大明神の宮司を務めたという{{R|角川1}}。 長く神埼一帯の総鎮守として崇敬されており、当社で保管されてきた古文書には平安時代後期の12世紀以降、度々朝廷や幕府、地元領主による造修が行われたことが記されている{{R|角川1}}。江戸時代には[[長崎街道]]が整備され、神埼宿が当社周辺に設けられた。 明治4年（1871年）に郷社、1880年（明治13年）に県社に列されている{{R|角川1}}。 == 祭神 == : 「櫛田三神」と称される。 * [[スサノオ|須佐之男命]] * [[クシナダヒメ|櫛稲田姫命]] * [[日本武尊|日本武命]] === 境内社 === * 櫛森稲荷神社 * 櫛丸稲荷神社 * [[祇園社]] * [[弁財天]]社 * [[天満宮]] == 主な祭事 == * 春祭 - [[4月7日]]・[[4月8日|8日]] ** 隔年で「みゆき大祭」と称し大規模に斎行される（西暦末尾偶数年の4月第一土・日曜日。[[2010年]]は[[4月3日]]・[[4月4日|4日]]）。御神幸や神楽奉納等が行われる。 * 秋祭 - [[10月7日]]・[[10月8日|8日]] == 文化財等 == '''佐賀県指定重要文化財'''（県指定有形文化財） * 石造肥前鳥居 - 柱と貫が3本継で慶長7年（1602）造立の銘がある初期の[[肥前鳥居]]。高さ3.35 m、笠木の長さ4.45 m。鍋島信濃守（[[鍋島勝茂]]）の記名がある。1964年5月23日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html#k_ju024 |title=県指定（建造物の部）01 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2021-08-19 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 '''佐賀県指定重要有形民俗文化財'''（県指定有形民俗文化財） * 御神幸祭[[絵馬]]（拝殿内） - 獅子舞（太神楽）を先頭に4基の神輿と行列が練り歩く神幸祭の様子を描いたもので、縦159cm・横184cmの大絵馬。「[[安政]]五戌午年八月吉祥日」の日付記載がある。1979年3月31日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html#k_yumi06 |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）01 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2020-06-23 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 '''佐賀県指定重要民俗文化財'''（県指定民俗文化財） * [[太神楽]]（だいかぐら） - 神幸祭（みゆき大祭）の「先払い」として行列の先頭で供奉される[[獅子舞]]。尾崎地区の住民によって行われ、現在は尾崎西分と尾崎東分で交互に担当している。獅子は緑色で長く30人ほどが入って交代しながら行う。垂れるほど目一杯の切紙で作った頭髪をまとい、道行しながら家々を廻る荒獅子では頭を振って邪気を払う。落ちた頭髪は安産や魔除けのご利益があるとされる。ほかに「もうし」、「ササラ」、太鼓、笛など総勢150名以上が出演する。1966年4月23日指定{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html#k_mumi12 |title=県指定（民俗文化財の部）02 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2020-06-23 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmCommunityPeopleDetail02.aspx |title=みゆき大祭 尾崎西分の太神楽 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |date=2012-03-30 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。 '''神埼市指定重要文化財'''（市指定有形文化財） * 櫛田宮文書{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmAboutDetail3.aspx |title=神埼市の指定文化財 |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |accessdate=2024-03-02}} - 「櫛田神社文書」として『佐賀県史料集成』古文書編第5巻（佐賀県立図書館、1960年）に収録 '''神埼市指定重要無形文化財'''（市指定無形文化財） * 締元行列 - みゆき大祭の御神幸に随従する。 '''記念物・樹木''' * 琴の楠 - 景行天皇が巡行の折に埋めた琴が[[クスノキ|楠]]になった伝説のある神木で、7回半周回すると「琴の音色が聞こえる」とされる{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=櫛田宮（くしだぐう） |website=神埼デジタルミュージアム「かんざき@NAVI」 |publisher=神埼市 |date=2012-03-13 |accessdate=2024-03-02}}。推定樹齢1000年。これを含めて境内には「佐賀県名木・古木」に登録されているクスノキが3本、[[イチョウ]]が1本あり、うちクスノキ1本とイチョウが推定樹齢700年を数える{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=佐賀県名木・古木 |work=佐賀県文化財データベース |publisher=佐賀県文化課 |date=2023-06-14 |accessdate=2023-08-10}}。 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[長崎本線]][[神埼駅]]下車、南西方向へ徒歩約10分。 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|group="注"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} == 関連項目 == * [[櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] * [[吉野ヶ里遺跡]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ 櫛田宮ホームページ]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=佐賀県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くしたくう}} [[Category:佐賀県の神社]] [[Category:佐賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:神埼市の歴史]] [[Category:佐賀県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:神埼市の建築物]] [[Category:景行天皇]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:佐賀県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市|12=draft}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|12=draft}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|12=draft}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘|12=draft}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|12=draft}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year|12=draft}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|12=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|12=draft}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|12=draft}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} {{citation needed|Kushida Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|qq=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|qq=draft}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|qq=draft}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘|qq=draft}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|qq=draft}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year|qq=draft}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|qq=draft}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|qq=draft}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|qq=draft}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] [[Category:refreshing redirects]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kanzaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki City|qq=}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|qq=}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki Manor|qq=}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitori Shrine|qq=}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1115 AD|qq=}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1602 AD|qq=}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeshima Katsushige|qq=}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1858 AD|qq=}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kanzaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kushida Shrine | native_name = 櫛田宮 | image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] | caption = Kushida Shrine | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture=Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|18|36.6|N|130|22|21.3|E|region:JP-41_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kanzaki 419-1, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} | established = Reign of Emperor Keikō | festivals = Spring festival (April 7–8)
Autumn festival (October 7–8)
''Miyuki Taisai'' (biennially in early April) }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, ''Kushida-gū'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki, Saga|ja|神埼市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki, Saga|qq=}}, Japan. It holds the historical status of a {{ill|prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the pre-World War II system of Ranked Shinto shrines. The Kushida Shrine in {{ill|Hakata, Fukuoka|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata, Fukuoka|qq=}}, is a branch shrine of this original location. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Keikō (early 4th century). The emperor, upon hearing of recurring misfortunes plaguing the area, ordered the enshrinement of local deities to pacify the land.Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten, "Kushida Shrine" entry. The surrounding region was named ''Kamisaki'' (神幸, later becoming Kanzaki), meaning "divine happiness," in commemoration of the shrine’s founding. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}, the sacred sword of Kushida Shrine was temporarily transferred to its branch shrine in Hakata for prayer rituals, which were believed to have aided in repelling the Mongol fleet. As one of the ''Sansho Daimyōjin'' (三所大明神), the shrine formed part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Shō|ja|神埼荘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki Shō|qq=}} triad along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitori Shrine|qq=}}. The present-day ''Miyuki Taisai'' festival still honors these three shrines with a grand procession of portable shrines (''mikoshi'') and other rites.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Taisai |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City}} == Deities == Kushida Shrine is dedicated to the "Kushida Sanjin" (櫛田三神): * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} * === Auxiliary Shrines === Several auxiliary shrines (''sessha'' and ''massha'') are located within the precincts: * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}} == festivals == * '''Spring festival''' (April 7–8): Includes the biennial ''Miyuki Taisai'' (held in even-numbered years), featuring elaborate processions and shrine rituals. * '''Autumn festival''' (October 7–8): Celebrates the harvest and includes traditional performances. == Cultural Properties== === Saga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties=== * '''Stone Torii Gate''': Constructed in 1602, this early example of {{ill|Hizen Torii|ja|肥前鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Torii|qq=}} architecture stands 3.35 meters tall, with a span of 4.45 meters. It was donated by {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeshima Katsushige|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties Database}} * '''Ema of the Miyuki festival''': A large votive plaque (159×184 cm) depicting the shrine's grand festival. Created in 1858 (''Ansei 5'').{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties Database}} * '''Daikagura Lion Dance''': Performed as part of the ''Miyuki Taisai'' procession, featuring an elaborate lion costume requiring 30 participants to carry it in shifts.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties Database}} == Notable Features == * '''"Koto no Kusu" Sacred Tree''': A legendary camphor tree believed to have grown from a musical instrument buried by Emperor Keikō. The tree is approximately 1,000 years old and emits "musical tones" when circled seven and a half times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Sacred Tree of Kushida Shrine}} == Access == * 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nagasaki Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Website] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kushidagu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Hizen Province]] [[Category:Japanese cultural properties]] }} [[sn:Kushidagu]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushidagu]] [[en:Kushidagu]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

Shrine (Fukuoka)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor Keikō]] }} [[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[en:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kushida Shrine | native_name = 櫛田宮 | image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] | caption = Kushida Shrine | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture=Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|18|36.6|N|130|22|21.3|E|region:JP-41_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | location = Kanzaki 419-1, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}}
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} | established = Reign of Emperor Keikō | festivals = Spring festival (April 7–8)
Autumn festival (October 7–8)
''Miyuki Taisai'' (biennially in early April) }} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, ''Kushida-gū'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki, Saga|ja|神埼市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki, Saga|qq=}}, Japan. It holds the historical status of a {{ill|prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the pre-World War II system of Ranked Shinto shrines. The Kushida Shrine in {{ill|Hakata, Fukuoka|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata, Fukuoka|qq=}}, is a branch shrine of this original location. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Keikō (early 4th century). The emperor, upon hearing of recurring misfortunes plaguing the area, ordered the enshrinement of local deities to pacify the land.Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten, "Kushida Shrine" entry. The surrounding region was named ''Kamisaki'' (神幸, later becoming Kanzaki), meaning "divine happiness," in commemoration of the shrine’s founding. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}, the sacred sword of Kushida Shrine was temporarily transferred to its branch shrine in Hakata for prayer rituals, which were believed to have aided in repelling the Mongol fleet. As one of the ''Sansho Daimyōjin'' (三所大明神), the shrine formed part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Shō|ja|神埼荘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki Shō|qq=}} triad along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitori Shrine|qq=}}. The present-day ''Miyuki Taisai'' festival still honors these three shrines with a grand procession of portable shrines (''mikoshi'') and other rites.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Taisai |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City}} == Deities == Kushida Shrine is dedicated to the "Kushida Sanjin" (櫛田三神): * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} * === Auxiliary Shrines === Several auxiliary shrines (''sessha'' and ''massha'') are located within the precincts: * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Benzaiten Shrine|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Tenmangu Shrine|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu Shrine|qq=}} == festivals == * '''Spring festival''' (April 7–8): Includes the biennial ''Miyuki Taisai'' (held in even-numbered years), featuring elaborate processions and shrine rituals. * '''Autumn festival''' (October 7–8): Celebrates the harvest and includes traditional performances. == Cultural Properties== === Saga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties=== * '''Stone Torii Gate''': Constructed in 1602, this early example of {{ill|Hizen Torii|ja|肥前鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen Torii|qq=}} architecture stands 3.35 meters tall, with a span of 4.45 meters. It was donated by {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeshima Katsushige|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties Database}} * '''Ema of the Miyuki festival''': A large votive plaque (159×184 cm) depicting the shrine's grand festival. Created in 1858 (''Ansei 5'').{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties Database}} * '''Daikagura Lion Dance''': Performed as part of the ''Miyuki Taisai'' procession, featuring an elaborate lion costume requiring 30 participants to carry it in shifts.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031562/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties Database}} == Notable Features == * '''"Koto no Kusu" Sacred Tree''': A legendary camphor tree believed to have grown from a musical instrument buried by Emperor Keikō. The tree is approximately 1,000 years old and emits "musical tones" when circled seven and a half times.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Sacred Tree of Kushida Shrine}} == Access == * 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Nagasaki Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Website] {{Shinto-navbox}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kushidagu]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Hizen Province]] [[Category:Japanese cultural properties]] }} [[sn:Kushidagu]] [[ja:櫛田宮]] [[sn:ushidagu]] [[en:Kushidagu]] [[Category:Merged pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kushida Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kushidagu Shrine (Kanzaki, Saga).jpg|250px]] |location = 419-1 Kanzaki, Kanzaki Town, Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture, Japan |ISO = JP-41 |Latitude = 33|LatMin=18|LatSec=36.6 |Longitude = 130|LongMin=22|LongSec=21.3 |LabelPos = bottom |Deities = Kushida Sanshin |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |Foundation = During the reign of Emperor Keikō |MainHall = |Alias = |Festival = April 7–8 (Spring Festival)
October 7–8 (Autumn Festival) |Rituals = Miyuki Grand Festival (biennially on the first Saturday and Sunday of April) }} {{maplink2|frame=yes|frame-width=200|frame-height=400|frame-lat=33.318|frame-long=130.374|frame-align=right|zoom=12|text=[[Kanzaki Manor]]'s Three Great Shrines
1. Kushida Shrine, 2. Koshi Shrine, 3. Oshitori Shrine |type=point|id=Q11544718|marker=-number|title=Kushida Shrine |type2=point|coord2={{Coord|33.28294|130.38718}}|marker2=-number|marker-size2=small|title2=Koshi Shrine |type3=point|id3=Q124716014|marker3=-number|marker-size3=small|title3=Oshitori Shrine}} '''Kushida Shrine''' (櫛田宮, Kushida-gū) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kanzaki City|ja|神埼市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki City|qq=}}, Saga Prefecture. It was formerly Ranked as a prefectural shrine. The famous {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|qq=}} is its branch shrine. == History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}} when he traveled to the area. Hearing of local misfortunes, the emperor ordered the shrine built to enshrine a deity and bring peace to the land.{{Cite book|editor=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 41.佐賀県 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |year=1982 |isbn=978-4-04-622957-1 |ref=角川 }} The name of the area, Kanzaki (神埼), is said to derive from the shrine's historical role in pacifying the region. During the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|ja|元寇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mongol invasions of Japan|qq=}}, the shrine's sacred sword was transferred to the Fukuoka Kushida Shrine, where prayers were conducted to repel the invaders. Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka was later established as a branch of this shrine. Kushida Shrine was one of three shrines revered as part of the {{ill|Kanzaki Manor|ja|神埼荘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanzaki Manor|qq=}}—along with {{ill|Koshi Shrine|ja|高志神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshi Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Oshitori Shrine|ja|白角折神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitori Shrine|qq=}}—known collectively as the Three Great Shrines (三所大明神). These shrines continue to participate in the Miyuki Grand Festival, with mikoshi (portable shrines) from each joining the celebrations.{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmDataDetail.aspx?id=11570 |title=Miyuki Grand Festival |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |publisher=Kanzaki City |accessdate=2024-03-02}} The shrine saw significant renovations under the order of Emperor Toba in {{ill|1115 AD|ja|永久3rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1115 AD|qq=}} and was supported by influential local families. It was designated as a Gōsha (village shrine) in 1871 and a Ken-sha (prefectural shrine) in 1880. == Deities == Known collectively as "Kushida Sanshin": * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime|ja|クシナダヒメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} === Auxiliary Shrines === * Kushimori Inari Shrine * Kushimaru Inari Shrine * Gion Shrine * Benzaiten Shrine * Tenmangu Shrine == Festivals == * '''Spring Festival''' - Held annually on '''April 7–8''' ** Biennially celebrated as the '''Miyuki Grand Festival''' (on even-numbered years, the first weekend of April). Includes mikoshi processions and kagura (sacred music and dance) performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' - Held annually on '''October 7–8''' == Cultural Assets == === Saga Prefecture Important Cultural Properties === * '''Hizen-style Stone Torii Gate''' - Built in {{ill|1602 AD|ja|慶長7th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1602 AD|qq=}}, features a distinctive design with three-part joints. Height: 3.35 m; crossbeam length: 4.45 m. Inscribed with the name of {{ill|Nabeshima Katsushige|ja|鍋島勝茂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeshima Katsushige|qq=}}. Designated in 1964.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031433/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Structures) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Important Folk Cultural Properties === * '''Goshinkō Festival Ema (votive tablet)''' - Large ema depicting the festival procession with mikoshi and lion dances. Dimensions: 159 cm × 184 cm. Dated to {{ill|1858 AD|ja|安政五年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1858 AD|qq=}}. Designated in 1979.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031554/index.html |title=Prefectural Cultural Properties (Folk Materials) |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2024-03-02}} === Significant Trees === * '''Koto no Kusu (Koto Camphor Tree)''' - A sacred camphor tree said to have grown from a koto buried by Emperor Keikō. Estimated age: 1,000 years. Circling the tree seven times is said to produce the sound of koto music.{{Cite web|url=https://www.kanzaki-museum.com/frmFeatureDetail.aspx?feature_id=4 |title=Kushida Shrine |website=Kanzaki Digital Museum |accessdate=2024-03-02}} Additional ancient trees include three camphors and one ginkgo over 700 years old.{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji00369053/index.html |title=Saga Prefecture's Notable Trees |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=2023-08-10}} == Access == * '''By Train:''' 10-minute walk southwest from {{ill|JR Kanzaki Station|ja|神埼駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kanzaki Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|ja|長崎本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagasaki Main Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Links == * [https://www5b.biglobe.ne.jp/~kusidagu/ Official Kushida Shrine Website] * {{ill|Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|櫛田神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushida Shrine (Fukuoka)|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田宮|version=102135443|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kushida Shrine (Saga)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kanzaki, Saga]] [[Category:Saga Prefecture Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Trees of Saga Prefecture]] [[Category:Emperor The Kushida Shrine in {{ill|Hakata, Fukuoka|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata, Fukuoka|qq=}}, is a branch shrine of this original location. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established during the reign of Emperor Keikō (early 4th century).
